title: SMISMEMBER
description: Determines whether multiple members belong to a set.
complexity: O(N) where N is the number of elements being checked for membership

returns.description: List representing the membership of the given elements, in the same order as they are requested

jedis.signature: List<Boolean> smismember(final byte[] key, final byte[]... members)

returns.description: List representing the membership of the given elements, in the same order as they are requested

jedis.signature: List<Boolean> smismember(final String key, final String... members)

content:
Returns whether each `member` is a member of the set stored at `key`. For every `member`, `1` is returned if the value is a member of the set, or `0` if the element is not a member of the set or if `key` does not exist. ## Examples {{% redis-cli %}} SADD myset "one" SADD myset "one" SMISMEMBER myset "one" "notamember" {{% /redis-cli %}} ## Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility | Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | | ## Return information {{< multitabs id="smismember-return-info" tab1="RESP2" tab2="RESP3" >}} [Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): a list representing the membership of the given elements, in the same order as they are requested. -tab-sep- [Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): a list representing the membership of the given elements, in the same order as they are requested. {{< /multitabs >}}
example:
SADD myset "one"
SADD myset "one"
SMISMEMBER myset "one" "notamember"
intro:
Returns whether each `member` is a member of the set stored at `key`.

For every `member`, `1` is returned if the value is a member of the set, or `0` if the element is not a member of the set or if `key` does not exist.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Returns whether each `member` is a member of the set stored at `key`.

For every `member`, `1` is returned if the value is a member of the set, or `0` if the element is not a member of the set or if `key` does not exist.
